R279_steel_pan_PTCH_within_head_harmonic.closest_adjacency_prior_art: DDSP harmonic-plus-noise model (Engel et al. ICLR 2020) — judge 0.62, different layer (audio output synthesis vs transformer attention-head weight SVD); Harmonic Convolution (Zhang et al. ICLR 2020) judge 0.55 different layer (CNN kernel frequency support); HingeNet judge 0.45; Harmonic Loss 0.30
R279_steel_pan_PTCH_within_head_harmonic.promoted_to: STRONGEST_NICHE_CANDIDATE_IN_CORPUS — flagged for maximum-visibility human review
R279_steel_pan_PTCH_within_head_harmonic.note: 37 cumulative cross-LLM queries spanning 20+ conceptual angles (LLM SVD, LoRA, music informatics, ML4audio, signal processing, quantization, harmonic loss, harmonic convolution, harmonic LLMs, eigenmodes, octave/tonnetz, vibration, coprime arrays, neural ODEs, DDSP, persona vectors). No single-paper hit reaches 0.7; no 3-5 paper L7 combination covers integer-ratio constraint without music metaphor. Mechanism mathematically distinctive: integer-ratio target spectrum on singular-direction magnitudes within attention head + explicit harmonic-alignment loss. UNCERTAIN-caveat kept due to (1) integer-ratio target arbitrary, (2) DDSP/Harmonic-Convolution use same integer-ratio at different layers, (3) music-DSP × neural-rep crossover venues under-indexed, (4) no empirical validation.
